title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Graph Lens: File History Renames
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git file history renames increment in a history-forensics evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Investigate path history, content movement, follow heuristics, and limitations without assuming Git stores rename events.
Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Investigate path history, content movement, follow heuristics, and limitations without assuming Git stores rename events. A defensible solution applies File History Renames, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-forensics evidence packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-file-history-renames — Investigate path history, content movement, follow heuristics, and limitations without assuming Git stores rename events.
git log --graph --decorate --oneline --all
git show --stat --summary HEAD
git cat-file -t HEAD^{tree}
git log --follow -- docs/notes.txt
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
